description:
You are a network engineer who has just finished collecting two troubleshooting outputs that already exist on the system:

1. /home/user/net_logs/ping_output.txt  
2. /home/user/net_logs/traceroute_output.txt  

Management policy says every ad-hoc diagnostic capture must be archived and accompanied by a small, single-line manifest so that an automated auditor can validate it later.

Your job:

A. Create a compressed archive called /home/user/archives/net_backup.tar.gz that contains **only** the two files listed above.  
B. Produce a manifest file called /home/user/archives/backup_manifest.log describing that archive.

Required directory: /home/user/archives (create it if it does not already exist).

Manifest format – four lines, UPPER-CASE keys, single equals sign, no extra spaces, exactly one line-feed between lines:

FILE_NAME=<exact file name of archive, must be net_backup.tar.gz>  
SHA256=<hexadecimal hash of the archive returned by sha256sum, 64 lowercase chars>  
SIZE_BYTES=<size in bytes of the archive as returned by the stat -c%s command>  
TIME_UTC=<current UTC timestamp in the form YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ>  

Example (for illustration only ­– values will differ):

FILE_NAME=net_backup.tar.gz  
SHA256=f2d2b0c22133b8c6c0a06e2e25b2b8262d0936fd3d90f9f959e5efcb4bcfabc8  
SIZE_BYTES=2048  
TIME_UTC=2023-09-15T14:32:07Z  

Verification rules the automated test will apply:

1. /home/user/archives/net_backup.tar.gz must exist.  
2. The archive must unpack to exactly two files whose paths end with ping_output.txt and traceroute_output.txt, and those files’ contents must be identical to the originals in /home/user/net_logs/.  
3. /home/user/archives/backup_manifest.log must exist and consist of exactly four non-empty lines that match the format above.  
4. The FILE_NAME line must be “FILE_NAME=net_backup.tar.gz”.  
5. The SHA256 line’s value must equal the actual SHA-256 hash of /home/user/archives/net_backup.tar.gz.  
6. The SIZE_BYTES value must be the correct byte size of the archive.  
7. TIME_UTC must be within ±2 minutes of the current system UTC time when the test runs.

Limit yourself to a simple sequence of 3–4 standard Linux commands to accomplish the task.
truth:
Initial state (already present before the agent begins):

Directory /home/user/net_logs/

1. /home/user/net_logs/ping_output.txt  
Contents (two lines, newline at end):  
PING 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8): 56 data bytes  
64 bytes from 8.8.8.8: icmp_seq=0 ttl=115 time=12.3 ms  

2. /home/user/net_logs/traceroute_output.txt  
Contents (three lines, newline at end):  
traceroute to 8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8), 30 hops max  
 1  10.0.0.1 (10.0.0.1)  1.123 ms  
 2  8.8.8.8 (8.8.8.8)  12.345 ms  

No /home/user/archives directory exists initially.

Expected final state after agent completes:

• Directory /home/user/archives/ exists.  
• /home/user/archives/net_backup.tar.gz exists and, when extracted, produces:
   net_logs/ping_output.txt (contents exactly as above)  
   net_logs/traceroute_output.txt (contents exactly as above)

• /home/user/archives/backup_manifest.log exists and satisfies rules 3-7 in the public description.

Automated test logic:

1. test -f /home/user/archives/net_backup.tar.gz  
2. tar -tzf /home/user/archives/net_backup.tar.gz | sort – expected two entries ending in ping_output.txt and traceroute_output.txt  
3. Compute sha256sum and stat size; read backup_manifest.log and compare values.  
4. Check TIME_UTC proximity.

Note: The manifest file is the only place the test will obtain the archive’s expected checksum, so no preset hash is required here.
name: task_000000_ed7d4b72
